originalTitle: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
title: Leading anti‑racism activist in Tunisia jailed for eight years
content: Saadia Mosbah campaigned for migrants, particularly after President Kais Saied said they posed a demographic threat to the country.

originalTitle: Desperate for food, drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
title: Desperate for food, drought-stricken Kenyans turn to the gingerbread tree
content: Some 26 million people are "facing extreme hunger" in Kenya, Ethiopia and Somalia, warns humanitarian organisation Oxfam.

originalTitle: UK agrees deal to ease migrant returns to Nigeria
title: UK agrees deal to ease migrant returns to Nigeria
content: It comes during the Nigerian president's state visit to the UK - the first by a west African leader in 37 years.

originalTitle: US judge sides with New York Times against Pentagon journalism policies
title: US judge sides with New York Times against Pentagon journalism policies
content: In his ruling, Judge Paul Friedman ruled the Trump administration sought to force out 'disfavoured journalists'.

originalTitle: Pezeshkian says Iran not seeking war with Muslim neighbours
title: Big News: A President Wants Peace, Not Fighting!
content:
Hi, amazing kids! It's your friendly African news reporter here, bringing you super exciting news from faraway lands! Today, we're going to talk about something very important: being friends and living in peace. It’s like when everyone in your class plays nicely together, but on a much bigger scale for whole countries!

Meet President Pezeshkian!

Imagine your school. You have a principal, right? Someone who helps everyone in the school get along and learn. Well, a country also has a special leader! This leader is often called a President. They have a very big and important job. Our news today is about a kind man named President Pezeshkian. He is the President of a country called Iran. Iran is a very big country, far, far away from us, with lots of people, homes, and schools, just like here in Africa! It has beautiful mountains and deserts, and many friendly people live there. President Pezeshkian has an important job. His job is to help all the people in Iran live happily and safely. He thinks about what's best for his country and its people every single day. It's a bit like being the head of a very, very big family.

Being Friends with Our Neighbours

Do you have friends who live next door to you? Or maybe friends in the next classroom? Those are like your neighbours! Countries also have neighbours. These are other countries that are very close to them on a map, sharing a border. It's super important for countries to be good neighbours. Just like you try to be kind to your friends next door, countries try to be kind to the countries next to them. This means talking to each other and helping each other. Why is it important? Because when neighbours are friends, they can help each other. They can share things, visit each other, and make sure everyone is safe and happy. Imagine if all your neighbours always smiled and helped you, wouldn't that be great?

Choosing Peace, Not Fighting

Now, here's the really, really good news from President Pezeshkian. He said that Iran does *not* want war with its neighbours. What does "war" mean? Imagine if you and your friends got into a big, big fight. Not just a little argument, but a really serious fight where no one is happy, and everyone feels sad or hurt. That's a bit like war for countries. War is when countries fight with each other. It's very sad, and it can hurt a lot of people and break many things, like homes and schools. Nobody wants war. It makes people scared, and it stops kids from going to school or playing outside. So, when President Pezeshkian says Iran is "not seeking war," it means they don't want to fight. They want to be peaceful. They want to be friends. They want everyone to live together without any big fights. Isn't that wonderful news? It means people can live without fear.

Did You Know? Fun Facts About Peace!

* **Did you know?** The word "peace" means a time when there is no fighting or war, and everyone is happy and calm. We all want peace in our homes, schools, and countries! * **Did you know?** Many countries in Africa work together to help each other and keep the peace. We believe in being good neighbours too! Our African Union helps countries talk and be friends. * **Did you know?** There's a special day every year called the International Day of Peace! People all over the world celebrate it to remind everyone to be kind and peaceful. It happens on September 21st! Maybe you can celebrate it too!

Why Peace is So Super Important

When countries choose peace, amazing things can happen. This is why President Pezeshkian's message is so important. Kids can go to school every day and learn new things without worrying. Families can play together in parks without being scared. People can build new houses and new roads, making their countries even better. Everyone can share their cultures, their yummy food, and their exciting stories with each other. It makes the world a richer and more interesting place! Think about it like this: If you and your friends always play nicely, you have more fun, right? You can build bigger sandcastles or play more exciting games. It's the same for countries! When they are peaceful, they can do so much good. When countries are peaceful, they can work together to solve big problems, like making sure everyone has enough food or clean water. They can even send help to other places when there is trouble, like floods or droughts. Peace helps everyone live a better life.

Friends with Muslim Neighbours

President Pezeshkian also talked about his "Muslim neighbours." What does "Muslim" mean? Well, just like some people go to church, or some people go to a temple, Muslim is the name for people who follow a religion called Islam. They have their own special ways of praying and their own special stories, just like people of different religions have theirs. Many countries around Iran have people who are also Muslim. So, when he talks about "Muslim neighbours," he's talking about countries where many people share the same religion. This can help them feel even more connected. It's like if your friends in your neighborhood all went to the same kind of school or played the same kind of sport. It makes them feel connected. President Pezeshkian wants to make sure all these connected countries can live as good friends, respecting each other.

Making Our World a Happy Place

So, what does this big news mean for you and me, all the way here in Africa? It means that leaders in faraway places are thinking about peace, just like we do! It reminds us that being kind and friendly is important everywhere in the world. Good ideas like peace can travel far, far away. Even though Iran is far away, the idea of not wanting to fight and wanting to be friends is something we all understand. We want our continent, Africa, to be peaceful too! And we want all the countries around the world to be peaceful. When leaders like President Pezeshkian say they want peace, it gives us hope. It shows that people are trying their best to make the world a happy, safe place for all children to grow up in. So, remember this important message, amazing kids: Being a good friend, sharing, and choosing peace over fighting are super important. Not just in your school or your home, but all around the world! Let's all try to spread peace and kindness every day. You can start by being kind to your friends and neighbours!
summary: The President of Iran, Mr. Pezeshkian, says his country wants to be friends with its neighbours and does not want to fight anyone, which is very good news for peace.
whyMatters: This is important because when countries choose peace, everyone is safer and happier, allowing kids to go to school, families to play, and people to build a better world without fighting.

originalTitle: Colombia’s President Gustavo Petro under investigation in US for drug ties
title: Colombia’s President Gustavo Petro under investigation in US for drug ties
content: Faced with reports that two US prosecutors have opened probes, the left-wing leader denies links to drug traffickers.

originalTitle: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
title: Iranian strikes on bases used by US caused $800m in damage, new analysis shows
content: Much of the damage was caused in initial retaliatory strikes by Iran in the week after the US and Israel launched its operation.

title: Norway's crown princess breaks silence, claiming she was 'manipulated and deceived' by Epstein
content: Crown Princess Mette Marit tells Norwegian TV she wishes she had never met the late sex offender.

originalTitle: Ukraine-Hungary oil pipeline row threatens EU loan
title: Ukraine-Hungary oil pipeline row threatens EU loan
content: Kyiv and Budapest disagree on how badly damaged a hub in western Ukraine bombed by Russia really is, as its oil flow remains suspended.

originalTitle: Maguire sent off as Bournemouth fight back to draw with Man Utd
title: Maguire sent off as Bournemouth fight back to draw with Man Utd
content: Despite taking the lead twice, Manchester United are held to a 2-2 draw at Bournemouth and have Harry Maguire sent off as they drop points in the race for a top-four finish in the Premier League.

originalTitle: One final experiment? Unpicking Tuchel's latest England squad
title: One final experiment? Unpicking Tuchel's latest England squad
content: Thomas Tuchel's expanded England squad offers hope to forgotten men but shows problems before the World Cup, writes Phil McNulty.

originalTitle: No contact, no World Cup place? What went wrong for Alexander-Arnold?
title: No contact, no World Cup place? What went wrong for Alexander-Arnold?
content: Trent Alexander-Arnold's omission from Thomas Tuchel's biggest England squad is the latest blow to his stop-start international career and casts huge doubt on his hopes of playing at the World Cup.
